is.t: CHECK THIS PLOT IS UNDER RAILWAY LINE

[09/07, 4:43 pm] Uthpalendu: The building is a G+1 structure, constructed in an apartment-style layout with identical ground and first floors. Two separate electrical connections with individual meters have been provided, making it suitable for use or rental by two families. In the deed schedule, the southern boundary is mentioned as adjoining Giri’s property. However, at the site, there is a private pathway on the southern side, after that Giri’s property. [09/07, 4:44 pm] Uthpalendu: The building has significant cracks and dampness on the first floor. The ground floor also shows minor dampness and some cracks.
LAND LOCKED ..NO ACCESS TO THE ABOVE PLOT

There are two properties with a total extent of 5.67 ares. One deed covers 1.62 ares under Resurvey No. 398/25, and the other covers 4.05 ares under Resurvey No. 398/5. Both deeds do not mention any access to the property in their schedule pages. However, at the site, there is an existing narrow pathway with a width of 0.95 meters providing access to the properties The first property, measuring 4.05 ares, has an old house with an asbestos sheet roof, currently in a dilapidated condition. The second property, measuring 1.62 ares, has a newly constructed building under development. The brickwork and the ground floor roof slab have been completed. However, plastering, electrical box installation, tiling, and sanitary fittings have not yet commenced. As of now, no construction work is actively in progress at the site following the completion of the roof slab.

The subject property is situated in a well-developed residential locality with good neighborhood profile and favorable surroundings. The building is east-facing and presently under commercial use, generating an average monthly rental/operational income of approximately ₹5,00,000. The structure includes a 4 sq. m. sheet-roofed portion, with the remaining built-up area constructed using standard materials and in good condition. Access to the property is via a 5.00-meter-wide Panchayat road, providing adequate frontage and smooth vehicular access. The site offers strong investment potential due to its high-income yield and strategic location.

As per the registered title deed, the property has an exclusive right of access via a 3-meter-wide private mud road, which is currently not in use. At present, the occupants are temporarily utilizing adjoining temple land for access. The asset comprises a south-facing residential building situated in close proximity to a temple, with a total plinth area of 275.32 m². The built-up area consists of 64.00 m² with tiled roofing, 22.00 m² with sheet roofing, and 10.00 m² forming the porch. The property is located within a well-developed residential zone, offering good environmental ambience and favourable vistas. The location has access to basic amenities and holds clear residential character, enhancing its suitability for mortgage evaluation.

The subject property is accessible via a private earthen road measuring approximately 3.00 meters in width. The existing structure is a north-facing building. The western boundary is presently without physical demarcation; however, the adjoining land on the west has been purchased by the owner but is pending registration.The property is situated in a predominantly residential locality with adequate natural ventilation and good visual exposure. The surrounding neighborhood includes a mix of residential dwellings and light industrial establishments, such as a plywood manufacturing facility and warehouse/godown units.

The subject property is approached through a private mud road having a width of approximately 3 (three) meters. The building situated thereon is oriented towards the East and lies at an approximate distance of 80 (eighty) meters from the commencement point of the Periyar River. Structural inspection reveals the presence of cracks in certain wall portions. On one side of the said private road, boundary demarcation is absent. The property further shares a staircase step with the adjoining premises. The site is located within a predominantly residential zone, enjoying adequate ventilation and favorable scenic surroundings.

"The subject property lacks visible or permanent boundary demarcations on all sides, which may require formal land survey and demarcation for precise identification of limits and to avoid potential boundary disputes in the future. Access is available via a Panchayat-classified road, measuring approximately 3.00 meters in width, as per on-site observation and local records. The principal structure is oriented towards the south. Due to restricted site accessibility, the inspection was limited to an external assessment, and the property’s location, orientation, and approximate extents were ascertained through geo-coordinate verification in conjunction with satellite imagery."

The subject property presently exhibits absence of boundary demarcation on three sides, with only the western boundary delineated by a foundation base. Access to the property is via a Panchayat-maintained roadway measuring approximately 3.00 meters in width. The building is oriented in a true north-facing alignment. Physical inspection was limited to an external survey, and the site particulars were verified through geo-coordinate mapping in reference to available cadastral records. No verification of internal structures or ownership boundaries was conducted, and the demarcation status is subject to confirmation through an approved land survey."

The property is accessible through a 3-meter-wide Panchayat road. The building is oriented towards the east. Boundary identification is limited to survey stones, with no permanent fencing or wall. The site is situated in proximity to a paddy field, and seasonal waterlogging is observed on the access road during the monsoon period."

Access to the property is via a 4-meter-wide Panchayat road. The building is west-facing and classified as a commercial property. The structure comprises approximately 8,500 sq. ft. under sheet roofing and 680 sq. ft. with RCC roofing, which is currently being used as an office space. As this is a commercial establishment, statutory approvals may be required from the Fire & Rescue Services Department, local Panchayat/Municipality, and other concerned authorities, depending on the nature of business operations, total built-up area, occupancy load, and applicable local regulations. It is advisable to verify and obtain necessary permissions such as Fire NOC, trade license, building completion certificate, and electrical safety approval, wherever applicable.
